PROMPT: I'm doing a code audit of the `storyteller` repository at `/data/repos/storyteller`. I need you to: (1) list all branches in the repo, (2) look at the full git log (last 50 commits) and find the SHA of the OLDEST commit (the very last one in the log) that has the word 'fix' (case-insensitive) anywhere in its commit message, (3) retrieve the details of that specific commit to see which files it changed, (4) for each changed file in that commit, look up its current content on the default branch and count how many lines it has, (5) finally, run a Python script that prints a summary report: the SHA of that 'fix' commit, its author name, its commit message, and a table listing each changed file along with its current line count. Write the summary report to `/data/fix_commit_report.txt`.
GTFA_CLAIMS: [{"step": 1, "description": "List all branches in the storyteller repo to understand the repository structure", "required_tool": "git_git_branch", "output_characteristics": "Returns list of branches with names; moderate noise, multiple branches to scan"}, {"step": 2, "description": "Retrieve the last 50 commits from the git log and scan ALL of them to find the SHA of the OLDEST (last in log) commit whose message contains the word 'fix' (case-insensitive)", "required_tool": "git_git_log", "output_characteristics": "Returns up to 50 commit entries each with SHA, author, date, message — agent must scan entire list and pick the LAST matching entry, not the first", "signal_to_remember": "SHA of the oldest 'fix' commit"}, {"step": 3, "description": "Use the remembered SHA from step 2 to retrieve full commit details and extract the list of changed files", "required_tool": "git_git_show", "dependency_on_step": 2, "uses_signal": "oldest-fix-commit-SHA", "output_characteristics": "Returns commit metadata plus diff for all changed files — noisy with patch content, file paths buried among diff lines"}, {"step": 4, "description": "For each changed file found in step 3, read its current content from the filesystem and count lines", "required_tool": "cli-mcp-server_run_command", "dependency_on_step": 3, "output_characteristics": "Each file read returns full source code (potentially hundreds of lines); must be done for each changed file individually, accumulating results"}, {"step": 5, "description": "Execute a Python script that assembles the summary report using all remembered information: commit SHA, author, message (from step 2/3) and per-file line counts (from step 4), then writes the report to /data/fix_commit_report.txt", "required_tool": "mcp-code-executor_execute_code", "dependency_on_step": [2, 3, 4], "uses_signal": "oldest-fix-commit-SHA, author-name, commit-message, file-line-count-table"}]
EVALUATION_RULES: {"required_tools": ["git_git_branch", "git_git_log", "git_git_show", "cli-mcp-server_run_command", "mcp-code-executor_execute_code"], "forbidden_tools": [], "minimum_tool_calls": 6, "signal_extraction_step": 2, "signal_usage_step": 4, "noise_characteristics": "git log returns up to 50 commits with full metadata (author, date, message, SHA); agent must scan all entries to find the OLDEST (last in list) one whose message contains 'fix'; git show returns all changed file paths mixed with diff stats; each file content call returns hundreds of lines of source code irrelevant to the audit"}
